rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELIPE R. VELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set-Aside The Order For Final Distribution (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2]Code of Civil Procedure section 473 provides for relief from a judgment, order, or other proceedings taken through mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 473, "The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading or proceeding [by correcting] a mistake in any other respect; []. The court may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars[.]" (sub. (a)(1).) Per subsection(d), "The court []may, on motion of either party after notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order." On the record before the court on this motion, the Order for Final Distribution issued on July 12, 2022, was obtained through mistake and extrinsic fraud - deception which prevents knowledge of one's rights, or prevents a party from having their day in court. (Estate of Sanders (1985) 40 Cal. 3d 607, 614.) Mr. Rivera told his legal counsel he would pay the anticipated statutory attorney's fees and court costs; Mr. Ibarra reasonably relied on this promise and filed the Petition for Final Distribution on the condition of this promise being honored. The promise has not been honored, despite the July 12, 2022 Order for Final Distribution directing the Administrator to do so, the apparent agreement between Mr. Rivera and his attorney to do so, the Ibarra Professional Law Corporation's demand letter sent to Mr. Rivera in July of 2022, and the Petition by the Ibarra Professional Law Corporation to Compel the Administrator to Pay the Statutory Fees Pursuant to the Order for Final Distribution, filed September 28, 2022 and supplemented and noticed January 11, 2023. The Order for Final Distribution issued on July 12, 2022, in this matter is hereby vacated and set aside on the basis of mistake, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELIPE R. VELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set-Aside The Order For Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Motion to Set-Aside the Order for Final Distribution. No opposition to this motion has been filed. Motion to Set-Aside the Order for Final Distribution is GRANTED. On May 10, 2022, Administrator Juan Rivera filed a verified "Amended First and Final Account and Petition for Final Distribution, and For Allowance of Compensation to Counsel. At page seven, lines 15 through 23, of the Petition, the Administrator stated that the estate consisted only of decedent's undivided 50% interest in 1298 Thomas Ave, San Francisco; the Administrator owned the other undivided 50% interest in the real property and did not wish to sell the real property in order to generate funds to pay the statutory attorney's fees and court costs. The Petition stated, "in order to pay the statutory attorneys' fees, and reimbursement to the Court for the costs previously waived [ ], the Administrator simply proposes to pay the $8,500 statutory fees and $1,336 reimbursement of Court costs out for his own pocket. Upon payment of the $8,500 statutory fees and $1,336 reimbursement of Court costs, the Administrator requests the Court to declare that the Decedent's undivided 50% interest of 1298 Thomas Ave. San Francisco, CA 94124 transfer to him." (Amd. Pet. at 7:15-23.) On the basis of this stated plan for payment of statutory attorney's fees and reimbursement of court fees by the Administrator and sole beneficiary, Administrator represented the estate was ready to be closed (Amd. Pet at Prayer, 7:24-8:10). Per the declaration of attorney Crisostomo G. Ibarra filed in support of the Motion, Administrator Juan Rivera informed his legal counsel, Mr. Ibarra, on several occasions between January 2019 and May 2022, during the probate estate administration process, that he would pay for the statutory attorney's fees and costs of administration from his personal funds. (Ibarra Decl. at 2:6-6-19.) Per Mr. Ibarra's declaration, the petition for final distribution was filed "on condition of Mr. Rivera's promise to pay such costs and fees without need to seek further court action to seek such compensation through sale of the estate asset." (Ibarra Decl. at 3:3-8.) An Order for Final Distribution issued on July 12, 2022. It ordered the Administrator, in relevant part to: (1) distribute the decedent's undivided 50% interest in 1298 Thomas Ave, San Francisco to Juan Rivera, as decedent's surviving brother; (2) pay the statutory fees of $8,500 out of the estate to the Ibarra Professional Law Corporation; reimburse the Court $1,336 for fees and costs previously waived, and pay $5.00 to counsel for File and Serve Express to file the subject petition for final distribution, out of the estate. (Order at par. 3, 4, 5.) As of the issuance of this tentative ruling, the Admistrator has not complied with the court's order to pay the statutory attorney's fees of $8,500 to the Ibarra Professional Law Corporation, or to reimburse the Court $1,336 for fees and costs previously waived, and pay $5.00 to counsel for File and Serve Express to file the subject petition for final distribution. (Ibarra Decl. at 3:12-16.) [Part 1 of 2]
